### 12.1.2General SBFD-capable BS Operating Requirements
An SBFD-capable BS may operate in either single-carrier mode or multi-carrier mode within a single band. In multi-carrier mode, SBFD shall be implemented on only one SBFD carrier regardless of the number of supported carriers within the frequency band.
An SBFD-capable BS shall only operate in SBFD subband configurations (i.e., DL/UL subbands) that have been declared in accordance with the provisions in this specification.
The RF requirements for SBFD-capable BS have been derived based on the simulation assumption that only SBFD-capable BSs are deployed with the identical SBFD configuration under each BS class in the same geographical area.
### 12.1.3SBFD-capable BS Types and Configuration Constraints
#### 12.1.3.1 General
SBFD-capable BSs are categorized as either configuration-limited or configuration-flexible according to the classification defined in Table 12.1.3.1-1. The classification is based on BS class and rated output power specified in clause 6.2.1.
Table 12.1.3.1-1: SBFD-capable BS classification
| BS Class | Rated Output Power (PSBFD,rated,c,sys) | SBFD BS type |
| --- | --- | --- |
| FR1 WA BS | -- | Configuration-limited |
| FR1 MR BS | ≥ 36 dBm | Configuration-limited |
| FR1 MR BS | < 36 dBm | Configuration-flexible |
| FR1 LA BS | -- | Configuration-flexible |
| FR2 WA BS | -- | Configuration-flexible |
| FR2 LA BS | -- | Configuration-flexible |
| NOTE 1:A power level threshold of PSBFD,rated,c,sys = 36 dBm is used to distinguish between configuration-limited and configuration-flexible SBFD-capable BSs in the FR1 MR BS class. |  |  |


##### 12.1.3.2Configuration-limited SBFD-capable BS
For configuration-limited SBFD-capable BSs, the following requirements apply:
For each channel bandwidth supported for SBFD operation, a maximum of three SBFD subband configurations may be declared. The three configurations shall cover all supported SBFD patterns, including both DUD and DU/UD cases. A single UL subband size used in both DU and UD configurations shall be counted as two configurations. For example, a UD 20-80 MHz and a DU 80-20 MHz configuration shall be counted as two SBFD subband configurations.
For each supported channel bandwidth and for each supported UL subband size: Only one DL subband size may be declared and tested for the DU/UD pattern. Only one DL subband size may be declared and tested for the DUD pattern.
A configuration-limited SBFD-capable BS shall not operate in any declared SBFD configuration (i.e., DL/UL subband) unless it has been tested.

##### 12.1.3.3 Configuration-flexible SBFD-capable BS
For configuration-flexible SBFD-capable BSs, the following requirements apply:
There is no restriction on the number of UL subband sizes that may be declared.
There is no restriction on the number of DL subband sizes that may be declared.
For each declared channel bandwidth and for each declared SBFD pattern (i.e., DUD or DU/UD), the following configurations shall be tested:
The configuration with the narrowest UL subband size, and the corresponding worst-case SBFD DL transmission bandwidth configuration:
For DU/UD: the configuration is the one with the largest NRB,SBFD,DL.
For DUD: the configuration is the one with the largest NRB,SBFD,DL,1 or with the largest NRB,SBFD,DL,2.
The configuration with the widest UL subband size, and the corresponding worst-case SBFD DL transmission bandwidth configuration:
For DU/UD: the configuration is the one with the largest NRB,SBFD,DL.
For DUD: the configuration is the one with the largest NRB,SBFD,DL,1 or with the largest NRB,SBFD,DL,2.
## 12.2 Conducted transmitter characteristics for SBFD-capable BS
### 12.2.1General
Conducted transmitter characteristics requirements apply on the SBFD-capable BS type 1-H including all its functional components active and for SBFD operation.
### 12.2.2Base station output power for SBFD
#### 12.2.2.1General
The BS conducted output power requirement for SBFD operation is specified over SBFD symbol, in which the rated carrier output power for SBFD symbol may be different from the rated carrier output powers for transmitter ON period as specified in clause 6.2.1.
#### 12.2.2.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-H
The same requirement as clause 6.2.3 is applicable to BS output power requirement for SBFD operation.
### 12.2.3Output power dynamics for SBFD
#### 12.2.3.1General
The requirements in clause 12.2.3 apply during SBFD symbol. Transmitted signal quality (as specified in clause 12.2.5) shall be maintained for the output power dynamics requirements of this clause.
#### 12.2.3.2RE power control dynamic range for SBFD
##### 12.2.3.2.1General
The RE power control dynamic range for SBFD operation is the difference between the power of an RE and the average RE power for a BS at maximum output power (PSBFD,max,c,TABC) during SBFD symbol for a specified reference condition.
For BS type 1-H this requirement shall apply at each TAB connector supporting transmission in the operating band.
##### 12.2.3.2.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-H
The same requirement as clause 6.3.2.2 is applicable to RE power control dynamic range requirement for SBFD operation.
#### 12.2.3.3Total power dynamic range for SBFD
##### 12.2.3.3.1General
The BS total power dynamic range for SBFD is the difference between the maximum and the minimum transmit power of an OFDM symbol for a specified reference condition.
For BS type 1-H this requirement shall apply at each TAB connector supporting transmission in the operating band.
NOTE 1:The upper limit of the total power dynamic range for SBFD is the OFDM symbol power for a BS when transmitting on all DL RBs in transmission bandwidth configuration for SBFD at maximum output power. The lower limit of the total power dynamic range is the average power for single RB transmission as non-SBFD. The OFDM symbol shall carry PDSCH and not contain RS or SSB.
NOTE 2:The requirement does not apply to operation with shared spectrum channel access.
##### 12.2.3.3.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-H
The downlink (DL) total power dynamic range for each NR carrier shall be larger than or equal to the level calculated as 10log10(NRB,SBFD,DL) if one SBFD DL subband is configured, i.e., DU or UD pattern, and as 10log10(NRB,SBFD,DL,1 + NRB,SBFD,DL,2) if two SBFD DL subbands are configured, i.e., DUD pattern.
### 12.2.4Transmit ON/OFF power for SBFD
#### 12.2.4.1Transmitter OFF power for SBFD
##### 12.2.4.1.1General
The general description for transmitter OFF power as clause 6.4 shall be applied.
##### 12.2.4.1.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-H
For the transmitter OFF power requirements for non-SBFD symbols, the same requirement as specified in clause 6.4 shall be applied. Requirement is not applicable for SBFD symbols.
### 12.2.5Transmitted signal quality for SBFD
#### 12.2.5.1General
The general description for transmitted signal quality as clause 6.5 shall be applied.
#### 12.2.5.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-H
For frequency error, modulation quality (EVM) and time alignment error (TAE), the same requirements as specified in clause 6.5 shall be applied.
### 12.2.6Unwanted emissions for SBFD
#### 12.2.6.1General
The general description for unwanted emissions as clause 6.6 shall be applied.
#### 12.2.6.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-H
For occupied bandwidth, adjacent channel leakage power ratio (ACLR), operating band unwanted emissions (OBUE), and transmitter spurious emissions the same requirements as specified in clause 6.6 shall be applied.
Requirements related with multi-band and non-contiguous spectrum are not applied to SBFD.
### 12.2.7Transmitter intermodulation for SBFD
#### 12.2.7.1General
The general description for transmitted signal intermodulation as clause 6.7 shall be applied.
#### 12.2.7.2Minimum requirements for BS type 1-H
For the transmitter intermodulation requirement, the same requirement as specified in clause 6.7 shall be applied. Receiver requirement is not specified during the transmitter intermodulation test.
### 12.2.8Transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD operation
#### 12.2.8.1General
Transmitter transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD operation requirements apply only to SBFD-capable BS.
The transmitter transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD operation is the time period during which the transmitter is changing from the transmitter OFF period to the transmitter ON period or vice versa. The transmitter transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD operation is illustrated in figure 12.2.8.1-1.
Transmitter output power frequency regions for transition Time Transmitter ON period (DL transmission) Transmitter OFF period Transmitter OFF period Transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD OFF power level  ON power level   UL transmission  GP or UL transmission  Transmitter output power frequency regions for transition Time Transmitter ON period (DL transmission) Transmitter OFF period Transmitter OFF period Transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD OFF power level  ON power level   UL transmission  GP or UL transmission
Figure 12.2.8.1-1: Example of relations between transmitter ON period, transmitter OFF period and transmitter transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD operation
The transmitter transient period for SBFD shall be measured in the transmission bandwidth configuration for SBFD DL subband, UL subband and guardband, depending on different cases as illustrated in table 12.2.8.1-1:
Table 12.2.8.1-1: Transmitter transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD operation
|  | Frequency regions for transition | BS transmitter behavior during transition |
| --- | --- | --- |
| SBFD symbol to DL symbol | SBFD UL subband and guardband(s) | BS transmitter OFF-to-ON |
| SBFD symbol to UL symbol | SBFD DL subband | BS transmitter ON-to-OFF |
| DL symbol to SBFD symbol | SBFD UL subband and guardband(s) | BS transmitter ON-to-OFF |
| UL symbol to SBFD symbol | SBFD DL subband | BS transmitter OFF-to-ON |


For BS type 1-H this requirement shall be applied at each TAB connector supporting transmission in the operating band.
#### 12.2.8.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-H
For BS type 1-H, the transmitter transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD operation shall be shorter than the values listed in the minimum requirement table 12.2.8.2-1.
Table 12.2.8.2-1: Minimum requirement for the transmitter transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD operation for BS type 1-H
| Transition | Transient period length (µs) |
| --- | --- |
| OFF to ON | 10 |
| ON to OFF | 10 |


### 12.2.9In-channel adjacent subband leakage for SBFD
#### 12.2.9.1General
In-channel adjacent subband leakage (ICASL) is the filtered mean power centred on the declared frequency region of SBFD UL subband within NR carrier.
The requirement shall apply during the SBFD symbol.
#### 12.2.9.2Limits and Basic limits
For SBFD operation in the unpaired spectrum, the ICASL shall be less than or equal to the value specified in table 12.2.9.2-1.
Table 12.2.9.2-1: SBFD Base station ICASL absolute basic limit
| SBFD BS category / BS class | ICASL absolute basic limit |
| --- | --- |
| Category A Wide Area BS | -13 dBm/MHz |
| Category B Wide Area BS | -15 dBm/MHz |
| Medium Range BS | -25 dBm/MHz |
| Local Area BS | -32 dBm/MHz |


Table 12.2.9.2-2: Filter parameters for the UL subband
| RAT | Filter on the assigned UL subband and corresponding filter bandwidth |
| --- | --- |
| NR | Square (the declared bandwidth of UL subband) |


#### 12.2.9.3Minimum requirement for BS type 1-H
The ICASL requirements are that for each TAB connector TX min cell group and each applicable basic limit in clause 12.2.9.2, the power summation emissions at the TAB connectors of the TAB connector TX min cell group shall not exceed a BS limit specified as the basic limit + X, where X = 10log10(NTXU,countedpercell).
NOTE:Conformance to the in-channel adjacent subband leakage requirement can be demonstrated by meeting at least one of the following criteria as determined by the manufacturer:
1)The sum of the in-channel adjacent subband leakage power measured on each TAB connector in the TAB connector TX min cell group shall be less than or equal to the limit as defined in this clause for the respective frequency span.
Or
2)The  in-channel adjacent subband leakage power at each TAB connector shall be less than or equal to the limit as defined in this clause for the respective frequency span, scaled by -10log10(n), where n is the number of TAB connectors in the TAB connector TX min cell group.
## 12.3Conducted receiver characteristics for SBFD-capable BS
### 12.3.1General
Conducted receiver characteristics are specified at the TAB connector for SBFD-capable BS type 1-H, with full complement of transceivers configured for SBFD operation in normal operating condition.
### 12.3.2Reference sensitivity level
#### 12.3.2.1General
The reference sensitivity power level is the minimum mean power received at the TAB connector for BS type 1-H inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation at which a throughput requirement shall be met for a specified reference measurement channel.
#### 12.3.2.2Minimum requirements for BS type 1-H
The same requirements as clause 7.2.2 is applicable to reference sensitivity power level for SBFD operation, in which the BS channel bandwidth specified in tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
### 12.3.3Dynamic range
#### 12.3.3.1General
The dynamic range is specified as a measure of the capability of the receiver to receive a wanted signal in the presence of an interfering signal at the TAB connector for BS type 1-H inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation. In this condition, a throughput requirement shall be met for a specified reference measurement channel. The interfering signal for the dynamic range requirement is an AWGN signal.
#### 12.3.3.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-H
The same requirements as clause 7.3.2 is applicable to dynamic range requirement for SBFD operation, in which the BS channel bandwidth specified in tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
### 12.3.4In-band selectivity and blocking for SBFD
#### 12.3.4.1Adjacent Channel Selectivity (ACS) for SBFD
##### 12.3.4.1.1General
Adjacent channel selectivity (ACS) is a measure of the receiver’s ability to receive a wanted signal at its assigned channel frequency at the TAB connector for BS type 1-H in the presence of an adjacent channel signal with a specified centre frequency offset of the interfering signal to the band edge of a victim system.
NOTE: SBFD UL subband is not configured adjacent to the Radio Bandwidth edge where cross link interference from adjacent channel may appear.
##### 12.3.4.1.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-H
For SBFD operation, the throughput shall be ≥ 95% of the maximum throughput of the reference measurement channel, with a wanted and an interfering signal coupled to BS type 1H TAB connector using the parameters in table 12.3.4.1.2-1 for ACS. The reference measurement channel for the wanted signal is further specified in annex A.1. The characteristics of the interfering signal is further specified in annex D.
The ACS requirement is applicable outside the Base Station RF Bandwidth or Radio Bandwidth. The interfering signal offset is defined relative to the Base station RF Bandwidth edges or Radio Bandwidth edges.
Table 12.3.4.1.2-1: Base station ACS requirement for SBFD
| BS channel bandwidth of the lowest/highest carrier received(MHz) | Wanted signal mean power (dBm) (Note 1) | Interfering signal mean power (dBm) | Interfering signal centre frequency minimum offset from the lower/upper Base Station RF Bandwidth edge or sub-block edge inside a sub-block gap (MHz) | Type of interfering signal |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45, 50, 60, 70, 80, 90, 100 | PREFSENS + 6 dB | Wide Area BS: -28Medium Range BS: -32Local Area BS: -35 | ±10 | 20 MHz CP-OFDM NR signal30 kHz SCS, 51 RBs |
| NOTE 1:PREFSENS for SBFD UL subband bandwidth depends on the BS channel bandwidth as specified in clause 12.3.2.NOTE 2:For lowest/highest carrier which is configured in DU/UD SBFD operation, the interference only applies when DL subband is adjacent to Base Station RF Bandwidth edge. |  |  |  |  |


#### 12.3.4.2In-band blocking for SBFD
##### 12.3.4.2.1General
The in-band blocking characteristics is a measure of the receiver's ability to receive a wanted signal at its assigned channel at the TAB connector for BS type 1-H in the presence of an unwanted interferer, which is an NR signal for general blocking.
NOTE 1: For FR1 WA BS, the in-band blocking requirements are derived based on the simulation assumption of 4GHz with 288 meters minimum distance coming from 100% grid shift between SBFD-capable BSs and neighbouring adjacent-channel TDD BSs. This NOTE shall not be interpreted as part of RF requirements.
NOTE 2: For FR1 MR BS, the in-band blocking requirements are derived based on the simulation assumption of 4GHz outdoor deployment with 167 meters minimum distance coming from 100% grid shift between SBFD-capable BSs and neighbouring adjacent-channel TDD BSs. This NOTE shall not be interpreted as part of RF requirements or in the context of indoor.
NOTE 3: SBFD UL subband is not configured adjacent to the Radio Bandwidth edge where cross link interference from adjacent channel may appear.
##### 12.3.4.2.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-H
The throughput shall be ≥ 95% of the maximum throughput of the reference measurement channel, with a wanted and an interfering signal coupled to BS type 1H TAB connector using the parameters in table 12.3.4.2.2-1 for general blocking for SBFD. The reference measurement channel for the wanted signal is further specified in annex A.1. The characteristics of the interfering signal is further specified in annex D.
The in-band blocking requirements apply outside the Base Station RF Bandwidth or Radio Bandwidth. The interfering signal offset is defined relative to the Base Station RF Bandwidth edges or Radio Bandwidth edges.
Minimum conducted requirement is defined at the TAB connector for BS type 1-H.
Table 12.3.4.2.2-1: Base station general blocking requirement
| BS channel bandwidth of the lowest/highest carrier received(MHz) | Wanted signal mean power (dBm) (Note 1) | Interfering signal mean power (dBm) | Interfering signal centre frequency minimum offset from the lower/upper Base Station RF Bandwidth edge or sub-block edge inside a sub-block gap (MHz) | Type of interfering signal |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45, 50, 60, 70, 80, 90, 100 | PREFSENS + 6 dB | Wide Area BS: -25Medium Range BS: -32Local Area BS: -35 | ±30 | 20 MHz CP-OFDM NR signal30 kHz SCS, 51 RBs |
| NOTE 1:PREFSENS for SBFD UL subband bandwidth depends on the BS channel bandwidth as specified in clause 12.3.2.NOTE 2:For lowest/highest carrier which is configured in DU/UD SBFD operation, the interference only applies when DL subband is adjacent to Base Station RF Bandwidth edge. |  |  |  |  |


### 12.3.5Out-of-band blocking for SBFD
#### 12.3.5.1General
The general requirement of out-of-band blocking for SBFD-capable BS is the same as general requirement specified in clause 7.5.1.
#### 12.3.5.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-H
The same requirements as specified in clause 7.5.2 shall be applied. The PREFSENS depends on the SBFD UL subband as specified in clause 12.3.2.
#### 12.3.5.3Co-location minimum requirements for BS type 1-H
The same requirements as specified in clause 7.5.3 shall be applied. The PREFSENS depends on the SBFD UL subband as specified in clause 12.3.2.
### 12.3.6Receiver spurious emissions for SBFD
#### 12.3.6.1General
The general description given in clause 7.6.1 for BS type 1-H shall be applied.
#### 12.3.6.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-H
The same requirements as specified in clause 7.6.4 shall be applied.
### 12.3.7In-channel selectivity for SBFD
The same requirements as specified in clause 7.8 shall be applied to SBFD-capable BS type 1-H during SBFD symbol, except for the note in table 7.8.2-1, table 7.8.2-1b,  table 7.8.2-2, table 7.8.2-2b, table 7.8.2-2d, table 7.8.2-3 and table 7.8.2-3d, which shall be adjusted to: Wanted and interfering signal are placed adjacently around FC,SBFD,UL. The aggregated wanted and interferer signal shall be centred in the SBFD UL subband bandwidth of the wanted signal. For DU/UD case, place the wanted signal closer to the DL subband.
### 12.3.8In-channel adjacent subband blocking for SBFD
#### 12.3.8.1General
The in-channel adjacent subband blocking requirement is a measure of the receiver’s ability to receive a wanted signal at its assigned channel frequency at the TAB connector for SBFD-capable BS type 1-H in the presence of an interfering signal from other sector(s) and/or site(s) within the same operator’s network, which is an NR signal.
#### 12.3.8.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-H
The throughput shall be ≥ 95% of the maximum throughput of the reference measurement channel, with a wanted signal and an interfering signal coupled to the SBFD-capable BS type 1-H TAB connector using the parameters specified in table 12.3.8.2-1. The reference measurement channel for the wanted signal is identified in table 7.2.2-1, 7.2.2-2 and 7.2.2-3 for each SBFD UL subband bandwidth and are further specified in annex A.1. The characteristic of the interfering signal is further specified in annex D.
The in-channel adjacent subband blocking requirement shall apply to SBFD UL subband bandwidth. The interfering signal(s) shall be positioned at the centre frequency of the SBFD DL subband bandwidth(s).
Minimum conducted requirement is defined at the TAB connector for BS type 1-H.
Table 12.3.8.2-1: In-channel adjacent subband blocking requirement for BS type 1-H
| SBFD UL subband bandwidth (MHz) | Wanted signal mean power (dBm) (NOTE 1) | Interfering signal mean power (dBm)(NOTE 5) | SBFD UD/UD or DUD case | Interfering signal centre frequency/frequencies | Type of interfering signal(s)（NOTE 2, 3, 4） |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45, 50, 60, 70, 80, 90 | PREFSENS + 6 dB | Wide Area BS: -28Medium Range BS: -32Local Area BS: -34 | DU or UD | FC,SBFD,DL | CP-OFDM NR signal30 kHz SCS, BW = X (in the unit of RB) |
|  |  |  | DUD | FC,SBFD,DL,1FC,SBFD,DL,2 | CP-OFDM NR signal30 kHz SCS, BW1 = X1 and BW2 = X2 (in the unit of RB) |
| NOTE 1:PREFSENS depends on the SBFD UL subband bandwidth as specified in clause 12.3.2. NOTE 2: For DU or UD case, interfering signal is placed at FC,SBFD,DL. For DUD case, two interfering signals are placed at FC,SBFD,DL,1 and FC,SBFD,DL,2, respectively, interfering signal mean power is the total power of the two interfering signals and the two interfering signals have the same power density.NOTE 3: For DU or UD case, X is the largest transmission bandwidth configuration as specified in table 5.3.2-1 that is not larger than NRB, SBFD, DL. For DUD case, X1 is the largest transmission bandwidth configuration as specified in table 5.3.2-1 that is not larger than NRB, SBFD, DL,1 and X2 is the largest transmission bandwidth configuration as specified in table 5.3.2-1 that is not larger than NRB, SBFD, DL,2.NOTE 4: In case the SBFD BS is configured with intra-band carrier aggregation, the set of interfering signals consists of the interfering signal(s) in this table and the signals from other intra-band contiguous carrier(s) operated in non-SBFD operation. The power spectral density of each interfering signal is set to be the same level.NOTE 5: For FR1 WA BS, in-channel adjacent subband blocking requirement assumes 80 dBc coupling loss between interfering BS transmitter and SBFD BS receiver. |  |  |  |  |  |


## 12.4Conducted performance requirements for SBFD-capable BS
### 12.4.1General
#### 12.4.1.1Scope and definitions
Conducted performance requirements specify the ability of the SBFD-capable BS type 1-H to correctly demodulate signals in various conditions and configurations. Conducted performance requirements are specified at the TAB connector(s) (for SBFD-capable BS type 1-H).
Conducted performance requirements for the BS are specified for the fixed reference channels defined in annex A and the propagation conditions in annex G. The requirements only apply to those FRCs that are supported by the base station.
Unless stated otherwise, performance requirements apply for a single carrier only. Performance requirements for a BS supporting carrier aggregation are defined in terms of single carrier requirements.
The SNR used in this clause is specified based on a single carrier and defined as:
SNR = S / N
Where:
Sis the total signal energy in the slot on a single TAB connector (for SBFD-capable BS type 1-H).
Nis the noise energy in a bandwidth corresponding to the transmission bandwidth over the same duration where signal energy exists on a single TAB connector (for SBFD-capable BS type 1-H).
#### 12.4.1.2Void
### 12.4.2Performance requirements for PUSCH
#### 12.4.2.1Requirements for PUSCH with transform precoding disabled
The same general description and requirements as clause 8.2.1 is appliable to requirements for PUSCH with transform precoding disabled, where the PUSCH received at the TAB connector for SBFD-capable BS type 1-H inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
#### 12.4.2.2Requirements for PUSCH with transform precoding enabled
The same general description and requirements as clause 8.2.2 is appliable to requirements for PUSCH with transform precoding disabled, where the PUSCH received at the TAB connector for SBFD-capable BS type 1-H inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
#### 12.4.2.3Requirements for UCI multiplexed on PUSCH
The same general description and requirements as clause 8.2.3 is appliable to requirements for UCI multiplexed on PUSCH, where the UCI multiplexed on PUSCH received at the TAB connector for SBFD-capable BS type 1-H inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
#### 12.4.2.4Enhanced performance requirements for PUSCH with synchronous interference
The same general description and requirements as clause 8.2.16 is appliable to requirements for enhanced performance requirements for PUSCH with synchronous interference, where the PUSCH received at the TAB connector for SBFD-capable BS type 1-H inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
#### 12.4.2.5Requirements for PUSCH with UL resource muting
##### 12.4.2.5.1General
The performance requirement of PUSCH with UL resource muting is determined by a minimum required throughput for a given SNR, where the PUSCH received at the TAB connector for BS type 1-H inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation. The required throughput is expressed as a fraction of maximum throughput for the FRCs listed in annex A. The performance requirements assume HARQ retransmissions.
Table: 12.4.2.5.1-1 Test parameters for testing PUSCH with UL resource muting
| Parameter | Value |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Tested signal | Interferer 1 (Note 1) | Interferer 2 (Note 1) |  |
| Transform precoding | Disabled |  |  |  |
| Default TDD UL-DL pattern (Note 2, Note 3) | 30 kHz SCS: XXXXXXXXUU |  |  |  |
| INR (dB, Note 4) | Set 1 | N/A | 9 | -1 |
| Interference Model (Note 5) | N/A | As specified in B.9.2 |  |  |
| HARQ | Maximum number of HARQ transmissions | 4 | N/A |  |
|  | RV sequence | 0, 2, 3, 1 | N/A |  |
| DM-RS | DM-RS configuration type | 1 |  |  |

|  | Number of DM-RS CDM group(s) without data | 2 |  |  |

| Time domain resource assignment | PUSCH mapping type | A, B |  |  |
|  | Start symbol | 0 |  |  |
|  | Allocation length | 14 |  |  |
| Muting resource assignement | Symbol positionng | 4 for mapping type A, 2 for mapping type B |  |  |
|  | CombOffset | 0 |  |  |
| Frequency domain resource assignment | RB assignment | 51 PRBs on the lower edge of full applicable bandwidth in X slots and full applicable bandwidth in U slots |  |  |

| Code block group based PUSCH transmission | Disabled |  |  |  |
| NOTE 1:Two explicit interferers are modelled for tests with 2, 4 or 8 RX antennasNOTE 2: The same requirements are applicable to TDD with different UL-DL patterns.NOTE 3: The sub-band PUSCH transmission start from the first X slot (TDD-UL-DL-ConfigCommon = ‘F’ for X slots). All symbols in X slot use sub-band PUSCH transmission NOTE 4:The respective received energy of each interferer relative to noise is defined by its associated INR value as specified in Annex B.9.1. The INR value is derived by only considering inter-site gNB-gNB interference using a link-budget method. Self-interference and intra-site inter-sector interference are not taken into account NOTE 5:All cells are time-synchronous. |  |  |  |  |


##### 12.4.2.5.2Minimum requirements
The throughput shall be equal to or larger than the fraction of maximum throughput for the FRCs stated in tables 12.4.2.5.2-1 at the given SNR measured only on X slots. FRCs are defined in annex A.

### 12.4.3Performance requirements for PUCCH
#### 12.4.3.1DTX to ACK probability
The same general description and requirements as clause 8.3.1 is appliable to requirements for DTX to ACK probability, where the UCI multiplexed on PUSCH received at the TAB connector for SBFD-capable BS type 1-H inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
#### 12.4.3.2Performance requirements for PUCCH format 0
The same general description and requirements as clause 8.3.2 is appliable to requirements for PUCCH format 0, where the PUCCH format 0 received at the TAB connector for SBFD-capable BS type 1-H inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
#### 12.4.3.3Performance requirements for PUCCH format 1
The same general description and requirements as clause 8.3.3 is appliable to requirements for PUCCH format 1, where the PUCCH format 1 received at the TAB connector for SBFD-capable BS type 1-H inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
#### 12.4.3.4Performance requirements for PUCCH format 2
The same general description and requirements as clause 8.3.4 is appliable to requirements for PUCCH format 2, where the PUCCH format 2 received at the TAB connector for SBFD-capable BS type 1-H inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
#### 12.4.3.5Performance requirements for PUCCH format 3
The same general description and requirements as clause 8.3.5 is appliable to requirements for PUCCH format 3, where the PUCCH format 3 received at the TAB connector for SBFD-capable BS type 1-H inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
#### 12.4.3.6Performance requirements for PUCCH format 4
The same general description and requirements as clause 8.3.6 is appliable to requirements for PUCCH format 4, where the PUCCH format 4 received at the TAB connector for SBFD-capable BS type 1-H inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
#### 12.4.3.7Performance requirements for multi-slot PUCCH
The same general description and requirements as clause 8.3.7 is appliable to requirements for multi-slot PUCCH format 1, where the multi-slot PUCCH format 1 received at the TAB connector for SBFD-capable BS type 1-H inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
### 12.4.4Performance requirements for PRACH
The same general description and requirements as clause 8.4.1 to clause 8.4.2.2 is appliable to requirements for PRACH, where the PRACH received at the TAB connector for SBFD-capable BS type 1-H inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.

## 12.5Radiated transmitter characteristics for SBFD-capable BS
### 12.5.1General
Radiated transmitter characteristics requirements apply on the SBFD-capable BS type 1-H, BS type 1-O or BS type 2-O including all its functional components active and for SBFD operation.
### 12.5.2Radiated transmit power for SBFD
#### 12.5.2.1General
The BS radiated transmit power for SBFD operation is specified over SBFD symbol, in which the EIRP for SBFD symbol may be different from the EIRP for transmitter ON period as specified in clause 9.2.1.
#### 12.5.2.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-H and BS type 1-O
The same requirement as clause 9.2.2 is applicable to BS radiated transmit power requirement for SBFD operation.
#### 12.5.2.3Minimum requirement for BS type 2-O
The same requirement as clause 9.2.3 is applicable to BS radiated transmit power requirement for SBFD operation.
### 12.5.3OTA base station output power for SBFD
#### 12.5.3.1General
The OTA base station output power for SBFD operation is specified over SBFD symbol, in which the TRP for SBFD symbol may be different from the TRP for transmitter ON period as specified in clause 9.3.1.
#### 12.5.3.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-O
The same requirement as clause 9.3.2 is applicable to OTA base station output power requirement for SBFD operation.
#### 12.5.3.3Minimum requirement for BS type 2-O
The same requirement as clause 9.3.3 is applicable to OTA base station output power requirement for SBFD operation.
### 12.5.4OTA output power dynamics for SBFD
#### 12.5.4.1General
The requirements in clause 12.5.4 apply during SBFD symbol. Transmit signal quality (as specified in clause 12.5.6) shall be maintained for the output power dynamics requirements of this clause.
The OTA output power requirements are directional requirements and apply to the beam peak directions over the OTA peak directions set.
#### 12.5.4.2OTA RE power control dynamic range for SBFD
##### 12.5.4.2.1General
The RE power control dynamic range for SBFD operation is the difference between the power of an RE and the average RE power for a BS at maximum output power (PSBFD,max,c,TABC) during SBFD symbol for a specified reference condition.
For BS type 1-H this requirement shall apply at each TAB connector supporting transmission in the operating band.
##### 12.5.4.2.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-O
The OTA RE power control dynamic range for SBFD operation is specified the same as the conducted RE power control dynamic range requirement for BS type 1-H in clause 12.2.3.2.
#### 12.5.4.3OTA total power dynamic range for SBFD
##### 12.5.4.3.1General
The BS total power dynamic range for SBFD is the difference between the maximum and the minimum transmit power of an OFDM symbol for a specified reference condition.
This requirement shall apply at each RIB supporting transmission in the operating band.
NOTE 1:The upper limit of the OTA total power dynamic range for SBFD is the BS maximum carrier EIRP (Pmax,c,EIRP) when transmitting on all DL RBs in transmission bandwidth configuration for SBFD at maximum output power. The lower limit of the OTA total power dynamic range is the average EIRP for single RB transmission in the same direction using the same beam. The OFDM symbol carries PDSCH and not contain RS or SSB.
##### 12.5.4.3.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-O and BS type 2-O
The OTA total power dynamic range minimum requirement for each NR carrier shall be larger than or equal to the level calculated as 10log10(NRB,SBFD,DL) if one SBFD DL subband is configured, i.e., DU or UD pattern, and as 10log10(NRB,SBFD,DL,1 + NRB,SBFD,DL,2) if two SBFD DL subbands are configured, i.e., DUD pattern.
### 12.5.5OTA transmit ON/OFF power for SBFD
#### 12.5.5.1OTA transmitter OFF power for SBFD
##### 12.5.5.1.1General
The general description for OTA transmitter OFF power as clause 9.5 shall be applied.
##### 12.5.5.1.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-O and BS type 2-O
For the OTA transmitter OFF power requirements for non-SBFD symbols, the same requirement as specified in clause 9.5 shall be applied. Requirement is not applicable for SBFD symbols.
### 12.5.6OTA transmitted signal quality for SBFD
#### 12.5.6.1General
The general description for OTA transmitted signal quality as clause 9.6 shall be applied.
#### 12.5.6.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-O and BS type 2-O
For frequency error, modulation quality (EVM) and time alignment error (TAE), the same requirements as specified in clause 9.6 shall be applied.
### 12.5.7OTA unwanted emissions for SBFD
#### 12.5.7.1General
The general description for OTA unwanted emissions as clause 9.7 shall be applied.
#### 12.5.7.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-O and BS type 2-O
For OTA occupied bandwidth, OTA adjacent channel leakage power ratio (ACLR), OTA operating band unwanted emissions (OBUE), and OTA transmitter spurious emissions the same requirements as specified in clause 9.7 shall be applied.
Requirements related with multi-band and non-contiguous spectrum are not applied to SBFD.
### 12.5.8OTA Transmitter intermodulation for SBFD
#### 12.5.8.1General
The general description for OTA transmitter intermodulation as clause 9.8 shall be applied.
#### 12.5.8.2Minimum requirements for BS type 1-O and BS type 2-O
For the OTA transmitter intermodulation requirement, the same requirement as specified in clause 9.8 shall be applied. Receiver requirement is not specified during the OTA transmitter intermodulation test.
### 12.5.9OTA spatial emission for SBFD
#### 12.5.9.1General
OTA spatial emission requirements apply to SBFD-capable BS during SBFD symbol.
#### 12.5.9.2Protection of FSS UL
For SBFD-capable BS type 1-H and BS type 1-O operating in band n104 during SBFD symbol, the Expected EIRP (EEIRP) in the frequency range 6425 – 7075 MHz, shall not exceed the limits specified in table 9.9.2.2-1.
### 12.5.10OTA transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD operation
#### 12.5.10.1General
The transmitter transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD operation is the time period during which the transmitter is changing from the transmitter OFF period to the transmitter ON period or vice versa. The transmitter transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD operation is illustrated in figure 12.5.10.1-1.
Transmitter output power frequency regions for transition Time Transmitter ON period (DL transmission) Transmitter OFF period Transmitter OFF period Transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD OFF power level  ON power level   UL transmission  GP or UL transmission  Transmitter output power frequency regions for transition Time Transmitter ON period (DL transmission) Transmitter OFF period Transmitter OFF period Transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD OFF power level  ON power level   UL transmission  GP or UL transmission
Figure 12.5.10.1-1: Example of relations between transmitter ON period, transmitter OFF period and transmitter transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD operation
The transmitter transient period for SBFD shall be measured in the transmission bandwidth configuration for SBFD DL subband, UL subband and guardband, depending on different cases as illustrated in table 12.5.10.1-1:
Table 12.5.10.1-1: Transmitter transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD operation
|  | Frequency regions for transition | BS transmitter behavior during transition |
| --- | --- | --- |
| SBFD symbol to DL symbol | SBFD UL subband and guardband(s) | BS transmitter OFF-to-ON |
| SBFD symbol to UL symbol | SBFD DL subband | BS transmitter ON-to-OFF |
| DL symbol to SBFD symbol | SBFD UL subband and guardband(s) | BS transmitter ON-to-OFF |
| UL symbol to SBFD symbol | SBFD DL subband | BS transmitter OFF-to-ON |


This requirement shall be applied at each RIB supporting transmission in the operating band.
#### 12.5.10.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-O
For BS type 1-O, the OTA transmitter transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD operation shall be shorter than the values listed in the minimum requirement table 12.5.10.2-1.
Table 12.5.10.2-1: Minimum requirement for the OTA transmitter transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD operation for BS type 1-O
| Transition | Transient period length (µs) |
| --- | --- |
| OFF to ON | 10 |
| ON to OFF | 10 |


#### 12.5.10.3Minimum requirement for BS type 2-O
For BS type 2-O, the OTA transmitter transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD operation shall be shorter than the values listed in the minimum requirement table 12.5.10.3-1.
Table 12.5.10.3-1: Minimum requirement for the OTA transmitter transient period between SBFD and non-SBFD operation for BS type 2-O
| Transition | Transient period length (µs) |
| --- | --- |
| OFF to ON | 3 |
| ON to OFF | 3 |


### 12.5.11OTA In-channel adjacent subband leakage for SBFD
#### 12.5.11.1 General
OTA In-channel adjacent subband leakage (ICASL) is the filtered mean power centred on the declared frequency region of UL subband configuration within NR carrier. The measured power is TRP.
The requirement shall be applied per RIB during SBFD symbol.
#### 12.5.11.2 Minimum requirement for BS type 1-O
The ICASL shall be less than or equal to a basic limit defined in table 12.2.9.2-1 + X (where X = 9 dB).
#### 12.5.11.3 Minimum requirement for BS type 2-O
The ICASL shall be less than or equal to the value specified in table 12.5.11.3-1.
Table 12.5.11.3-1: SBFD Base station ICASL absolute basic limit
| SBFD BS category / BS class | ICASL absolute basic limit |
| --- | --- |
| Wide Area BS | -13 dBm/MHz |
| Medium Range BS | -20 dBm/MHz |
| Local Area BS | -20 dBm/MHz |


Table 12.5.11.3-2: Filter parameters for the UL subband
| RAT | Filter on the assigned UL subband and corresponding filter bandwidth |
| --- | --- |
| NR | Square (the declared bandwidth of UL subband) |


## 12.6 Radiated receiver characteristics for SBFD-capable BS
### 12.6.1General
Radiated receiver characteristics are specified at RIB for BS type 1-H, BS type 1-O, or BS type 2-O, with full complement of transceivers configured for SBFD operation in normal operating condition.
### 12.6.2OTA sensitivity for SBFD
#### 12.6.2.1BS type 1-H and BS type 1-O
##### 12.6.2.1.1General
The general description of OTA sensitivity requirement as clause 10.2.1.1 shall also be applied for SBFD operation.
##### 12.6.2.1.2Minimum requirement
For a received signal whose AoA of the incident wave is within the active sensitivity RoAoA of an OSDD, the error rate criterion as described in clause 7.2 shall be met when the level of the arriving signal is equal to the minimum EIS level with 0.7dB degradation allowed in the respective declared set of EIS level and UL subband bandwidth, in SBFD symbols with the transmitted signals in SBFD DL subband(s) present.
### 12.6.3OTA reference sensitivity level for SBFD
#### 12.6.3.1General
The general description of OTA reference sensitivity requirement as clause 10.3.1 shall also be applied for SBFD operation.
#### 12.6.3.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-O
The OTA reference sensitivity requirement as specified in clause 10.3.2 with 0.7dB degradation allowed is applicable for SBFD operation in SBFD symbols with the transmitted signals in SBFD DL subband(s) present, in which the BS channel bandwidth specified in tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
#### 12.6.3.3Minimum requirement for BS type 2-O
The OTA reference sensitivity requirement as specified in clause 10.3.3 with 0.7dB degradation allowed is applicable for SBFD operation in SBFD symbols with the transmitted signals in SBFD DL subband(s) present, in which the BS channel bandwidth specified in tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
### 12.6.4OTA dynamic range for SBFD
#### 12.6.4.1General
The general description of OTA dynamic range requirement as clause 10.4.1 shall also be applied for SBFD operation.
#### 12.6.4.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-O
The same requirement as clause 10.4.2 is applicable to dynamic range requirement for SBFD operation, in which the BS channel bandwidth specified in tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
### 12.6.5OTA in-band selectivity and blocking for SBFD
#### 12.6.5.1OTA Adjacent Channel Selectivity for SBFD
##### 12.6.5.1.1General
OTA Adjacent channel selectivity (ACS) is a measure of the receiver's ability to receive an OTA wanted signal at its assigned channel frequency in the presence of an OTA adjacent channel signal with a specified centre frequency offset of the interfering signal to the band edge of a victim system.
NOTE: SBFD UL subband is not configured adjacent to the Radio Bandwidth edge where cross link interference from adjacent channel may appear.
##### 12.6.5.1.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-O
The requirement shall apply at the RIB when the AoA of the incident wave of a received signal and the interfering signal are from the same direction and are within the minSENS RoAoA.
The wanted and interfering signals apply to each supported polarization, under the assumption of polarization match.
The throughput shall be ≥ 95% of the maximum throughput of the reference measurement channel.
For SBFD operation, the OTA wanted and the interfering signal are specified in table 12.6.5.1.2-1 for OTA ACS. The reference measurement channel for the OTA wanted signal is further specified in annex A.1. The characteristic of the interfering signal is further specified in annex D.
The OTA ACS requirement is applicable outside the Base Station RF Bandwidth or Radio Bandwidth. The OTA interfering signal offset is defined relative to the Base station RF Bandwidth edges or Radio Bandwidth edges.
Table 12.6.5.1.2-1: OTA ACS requirement for BS type 1-O for SBFD
| BS channel bandwidth of the lowest/highest carrier received(MHz) | Wanted signal mean power (dBm) (Note 1) | Interfering signal mean power (dBm) | Interfering signal centre frequency minimum offset from the lower/upper Base Station RF Bandwidth edge or sub-block edge inside a sub-block gap (MHz) | Type of interfering signal |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45, 50, 60, 70, 80, 90, 100 | EISREFSENS + 6 dB | Wide Area BS: -28 - ΔOTAREFSENSMedium Range BS: -32 - ΔOTAREFSENSLocal Area BS: -35 - ΔOTAREFSENS | ±10 | 20 MHz CP-OFDM NR signal30 kHz SCS, 51 RBs |
| NOTE 1:EISREFSENS is given in clause 12.6.3.NOTE 2:For lowest/highest carrier is configured in DU/UD SBFD operation, the interference only applies when DL subband is adjacent to Base Station RF Bandwidth edge. |  |  |  |  |


##### 12.6.5.1.3Minimum requirement for BS type 2-O
The general minimum requirements are the same as those specified in clause 10.5.1.3, except for EISREFSENS. The EISREFSENS depends on the SBFD-capable BS class and the SBFD UL subband, and it is specified in clause 12.6.3.
#### 12.6.5.2OTA in-band blocking for SBFD
##### 12.6.5.2.1General
The OTA in-band blocking characteristics is a measure of the receiver's ability to receive an OTA wanted signal at its assigned channel in the presence of an unwanted OTA interferer, which is an NR signal for general blocking or an NR signal with one RB for narrowband blocking.
NOTE 1: For FR1 WA BS, the in-band blocking requirements are derived based on the simulation assumption of 4GHz with 288 meters minimum distance coming from 100% grid shift between SBFD-capable BSs and neighbouring adjacent-channel TDD BSs. This NOTE shall not be interpreted as part of RF requirements.
NOTE 2: For FR1 MR BS, the in-band blocking requirements are derived based on the simulation assumption of 4GHz outdoor deployment with 167 meters minimum distance coming from 100% grid shift between SBFD-capable BSs and neighbouring adjacent-channel TDD BSs. This NOTE shall not be interpreted as part of RF requirements or in the context of indoor.
NOTE 3: SBFD UL subband is not configured adjacent to the Radio Bandwidth edge where cross link interference from adjacent channel may appear.
NOTE 4: For FR2-1 WA BS, the in-band blocking requirements are derived based on the simulation assumption of 30GHz with 115 meters minimum distance coming from 100% grid shift between SBFD-capable BSs and neighbouring adjacent-channel TDD BSs. This NOTE shall not be interpreted as part of RF requirements.
##### 12.6.5.2.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-O
The requirement shall apply at the RIB when the AoA of the incident wave of a received signal and the interfering signal are from the same direction, and:
-when the wanted signal is based on EISREFSENS: the AoA of the incident wave of a received signal and the interfering signal are within the OTA REFSENS RoAoA.
-when the wanted signal is based on EISminSENS: the AoA of the incident wave of a received signal and the interfering signal are within the minSENS RoAoA.
The wanted and interfering signals apply to each supported polarization, under the assumption of polarization match.
The throughput shall be ≥ 95% of the maximum throughput of the reference measurement channel, with OTA wanted and OTA interfering signal specified in tables 12.6.5.2.2-1 for general OTA blocking requirements. The reference measurement channel for the OTA wanted signal is further specified in annex A.1. The characteristics of the interfering signal is further specified in annex D.
The OTA in-band blocking requirements apply outside the Base Station RF Bandwidth or Radio Bandwidth. The interfering signal offset is defined relative to the Base Station RF Bandwidth edges or Radio Bandwidth edges.
Table 12.6.5.2.2-1: General OTA blocking requirement for BS type 1-O
| BS channel bandwidth of the lowest/highest carrier received(MHz) | Wanted signal mean power (dBm) (Note 1) | Interfering signal mean power (dBm) | Interfering signal centre frequency minimum offset from the lower/upper Base Station RF Bandwidth edge or sub-block edge inside a sub-block gap (MHz) | Type of interfering signal |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45, 50, 60, 70, 80, 90, 100 | EISREFSENS + 6 dB | Wide Area BS: -25 - ΔOTAREFSENSMedium Range BS: -32 - ΔOTAREFSENSLocal Area BS: -35 - ΔOTAREFSENS | ±30 | 20 MHz CP-OFDM NR signal30 kHz SCS, 51 RBs |
|  | EISminSENS + 6 dB | Wide Area BS: -25 - ΔminSENSMedium Range BS: -32 - ΔminSENSLocal Area BS: -35 - ΔminSENS |  |  |
| NOTE 1:EISREFSENS is given in clause 12.6.3.NOTE 2:For lowest/highest carrier which is configured in DU/UD SBFD operation, the interference only applies when DL subband is adjacent to Base Station RF Bandwidth edge. |  |  |  |  |


##### 12.6.5.2.3Minimum requirement for BS type 2-O
The requirement shall apply at the RIB when the AoA of the incident wave of a received signal and the interfering signal are from the same direction and are within the OTA REFSENS RoAoA.
The wanted and interfering signals apply to each supported polarization, under the assumption of polarization match.
The throughput shall be ≥ 95% of the maximum throughput of the reference measurement channel.
For BS type 2-O, the OTA wanted and OTA interfering signals are provided at RIB using the parameters in table 12.6.5.2.2-1 for general OTA blocking requirements. The reference measurement channel for the wanted signal is further specified in annex A.1. The characteristics of the interfering signal is further specified in annex D.
The OTA blocking requirements are applicable outside the Base Station RF Bandwidth. The interfering signal offset is defined relative to the Base Station RF Bandwidth edges.
Table 12.6.5.2.3-1: General OTA blocking requirement for BS type 2-O
| Frequency Range | BS channel bandwidth of the lowest/highest carrier received (MHz) | OTA wanted signal mean power (dBm) | OTA interfering signal mean power (dBm) | OTA interfering signal centre frequency offsetfrom the lower/upper Base Station RF Bandwidth edge (MHz) | Type of OTA interfering signal |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FR2-1 | 100, 200, 400 | EISREFSENS + 6 dB | EISREFSENS_50M + 37 + ΔFR2_REFSENS | ±75 | 50 MHz DFT-s-OFDM NR signal,60 kHz SCS, 64 RBs |
| NOTE 1:EISREFSENS and EISREFSENS_50M are given in clause 12.6.3.NOTE 2:For lowest/highest carrier which is configured in DU/UD SBFD operation, the interference only applies when DL subband is adjacent to Base Station RF Bandwidth edge. |  |  |  |  |  |


### 12.6.6OTA out-of-band blocking for SBFD
#### 12.6.6.1General
The general requirement of OTA out-of-band blocking for SBFD-capable BS is the same as general requirement specified in clause 10.6.1.
#### 12.6.6.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-O
##### 12.6.6.2.1General minimum requirement
The general minimum requirements are the same as those specified in clause 10.6.2.1, except for EISminSENS. The EISminSENS depends on the SBFD-capable BS class and the SBFD UL subband, and it is specified in clause 12.6.2.
##### 12.6.6.2.2Co-location minimum requirement
The co-location minimum requirements are the same as those specified in clause 10.6.2.2, except for EISminSENS. The EISminSENS depends on the SBFD-capable BS class and the SBFD UL subband, and it is specified in clause 12.6.2.
#### 12.6.6.3Minimum requirement for BS type 2-O
##### 12.6.6.3.1General minimum requirement
The general minimum requirements are the same as those specified in clause 10.6.3.1, except for EISREFSENS. The EISREFSENS depends on the SBFD-capable BS class and the SBFD UL subband, and it is specified in clause 12.6.3.
### 12.6.7OTA receiver spurious emissions for SBFD
#### 12.6.7.1General
The general description given in Clause 10.7.1 shall be applied.
#### 12.6.7.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-O
The same requirements as specified in Clause 10.7.2 shall be applied.
#### 12.6.7.3Minimum requirement for BS type 2-O
The same requirements as specified in Clause 10.7.3 shall be applied.
### 12.6.8OTA in-channel selectivity for SBFD
The same requirements as specified in clause 10.9 shall be applied to SBFD-capable BS type 1-O and BS type 2-O during SBFD symbol, except for the note in table 10.9.2-1, table 10.9.2-1a, table 10.9.2-2, table 10.9.2-2a, table 10.9.2-3 and table 10.9.2-3a, which shall be adjusted to: Wanted and interfering signal are placed adjacently around FC,SBFD,UL. The aggregated wanted and interferer signal shall be centred in the SBFD UL subband bandwidth of the wanted signal. For DU/UD pattern, place the wanted signal closer to the DL subband.
### 12.6.9OTA in-channel adjacent subband blocking for SBFD
#### 12.6.9.1General
OTA in-channel adjacent subband blocking requirement is a measure of the receiver’s ability to receive an OTA wanted signal at its assigned channel frequency in the presence of an OTA interfering signal from other sector(s) and/or site(s) within the same operator’s network, which is an NR signal.
#### 12.6.9.2Minimum requirement for BS type 1-O
The requirement shall apply at the RIB when the AoA of the incident wave of a received signal and the interfering signal are from the same direction, and:
-when the wanted signal is based on EISREFSENS: the AoA of the incident wave of a received signal and the interfering signal are within the OTA REFSENS RoAoA.
-when the wanted signal is based on EISminSENS: the AoA of the incident wave of a received signal and the interfering signal are within the minSENS RoAoA.
The wanted and interfering signals apply to each supported polarization, under the assumption of polarization match.
The throughput shall be ≥ 95% of the maximum throughput of the reference measurement channel, with an OTA wanted signal and OTA interfering signal(s) using the parameters specified in table 12.6.9.2-1. The reference measurement channel for the wanted signal is identified in clause 10.3.2 for each SBFD UL subband bandwidth and are further specified in annex A.1. The characteristic of the interfering signal(s) is further specified in annex D.
The OTA in-channel adjacent subband blocking requirement shall apply to SBFD UL subband bandwidth. The interfering signal(s) shall be positioned at the centre frequency of the SBFD DL subband bandwidth(s).
Table 12.6.9.2-1: In-channel adjacent subband blocking requirement for BS type 1-O
| SBFD UL subband bandwidth (MHz) | Wanted signal mean power (dBm) (NOTE 1) | Interfering signal mean power (dBm)(NOTE 5) | SBFD UD/UD or DUD case | Interfering signal centre frequency/frequencies | Type of interfering signal(s)（NOTE 2, 3, 4） |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45, 50, 60, 70, 80, 90 | EISREFSENS + 6 dB | Wide Area BS: -28 - ΔOTAREFSENSMedium Range BS: -32 - ΔOTAREFSENSLocal Area BS: -34 - ΔOTAREFSENS | DU or UD | FC,SBFD,DL | CP-OFDM NR signal30 kHz SCS, BW = X (in the unit of RB) |
|  |  |  | DUD | FC,SBFD,DL,1FC,SBFD,DL,2 | CP-OFDM NR signal30 kHz SCS, BW1 = X1 and BW2 = X2 (in the unit of RB) |
|  | EISminSENS + 6 dB | Wide Area BS: -28 - ΔminSENSMedium Range BS: -32 - ΔminSENSLocal Area BS: -34 - ΔminSENS | DU or UD | FC,SBFD,DL | CP-OFDM NR signal30 kHz SCS, BW = X (in the unit of RB) |
|  |  |  | DUD | FC,SBFD,DL,1FC,SBFD,DL,2 | CP-OFDM NR signal30 kHz SCS, BW1 = X1 and BW2 = X2 (in the unit of RB) |
| NOTE 1:PREFSENS depends on the SBFD UL subband bandwidth as specified in clause 12.3.2. NOTE 2:For DU or UD case, interfering signal is placed at FC,SBFD,DL. For DUD case, two interfering signals are placed at FC,SBFD,DL,1 and FC,SBFD,DL,2, respectively. OTA interfering signal mean power is the total power of the two interfering signals and the two interfering signals have the same power density.NOTE 3:For DU or UD case, X is the largest transmission bandwidth configuration as specified in table 5.3.2-1 that is not larger than NRB, SBFD, DL. For DUD case, X1 is the largest transmission bandwidth configuration as specified in table 5.3.2-1 that is not larger than NRB, SBFD, DL,1 and X2 is the largest transmission bandwidth configuration as specified in table 5.3.2-1 that is not larger than NRB, SBFD, DL,2.NOTE 4:In case the SBFD BS is configured with intra-band carrier aggregation, the set of interfering signals consists of the interfering signal(s) in this table and the signals from other intra-band contiguous carrier(s) operated in non-SBFD operation. The power spectral density of each interfering signal is set to be the same level.NOTE 5:For FR1 WA BS, in-channel adjacent subband blocking requirement assumes 80 dBc coupling loss between interfering BS transmitter and SBFD BS receiver. |  |  |  |  |  |


#### 12.6.9.3Minimum requirement for BS type 2-O
The requirement shall apply at the RIB when the AoA of the incident wave of a received signal and the interfering signal are from the same direction and are within the OTA REFSENS RoAoA.
The wanted and interfering signals apply to each supported polarization, under the assumption of polarization match.
The throughput shall be ≥ 95% of the maximum throughput of the reference measurement channel, with an OTA wanted signal and OTA interfering signal(s) using the parameters specified in table 12.6.9.3-1. The reference measurement channel for the wanted signal is further specified in annex A.1. The characteristic of the interfering signal(s) is further specified in annex D.
The OTA in-channel adjacent subband blocking requirement shall apply to SBFD UL subband bandwidth. The interfering signal(s) shall be positioned at the centre frequency of the SBFD DL subband bandwidth(s).
Table 12.6.9.3-1: In-channel adjacent subband blocking requirement for BS type 2-O
| Frequency Range | SBFD UL subband bandwidth (MHz) | OTA wanted signal mean power (dBm)(NOTE 1) | OTA interfering signal mean power (dBm)(NOTE 5) | SBFD UD/UD or DUD case | Interfering signal centre frequency/frequencies | Type of interfering signal(s)（NOTE 2, 3, 4） |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FR2-1 | 50, 100, 200 | EISREFSENS + 6 dB | Wide Area BS: EISREFSENS_50M + 34 + ΔFR2_REFSENS | DU or UD | FC,SBFD,DL | CP-OFDM NR signal60 kHz SCS, BW = X (in the unit of RB) |
|  |  |  |  | DUD | FC,SBFD,DL,1FC,SBFD,DL,2 | CP-OFDM NR signal60 kHz SCS, BW1 = X1 and BW2 = X2 (in the unit of RB) |
| NOTE 1:EISREFSENS depends on the SBFD BS UL subband bandwidth as specified in clause 12.6.3. NOTE 2:For DU or UD case, interfering signal is placed at FC,SBFD,DL. For DUD case, two interfering signals are placed at FC,SBFD,DL,1 and FC,SBFD,DL,2, respectively, OTA interfering signal mean power is the total power of the two interfering signals and the two interfering signals have the same power densityNOTE 3:For DU or UD case, X is the largest transmission bandwidth configuration as specified in table 5.3.2-2 that is not larger than NRB, SBFD, DL. For DUD case, X1 is the largest transmission bandwidth configuration as specified in table 5.3.2-2 that is not larger than NRB, SBFD, DL,1 and X2 is the largest transmission bandwidth configuration as specified in table 5.3.2-2 that is not larger than NRB, SBFD, DL,2.NOTE 4:In case the SBFD BS is configured with intra-band carrier aggregation, the set of interfering signals consists of the interfering signal(s) in this table and the signals from other intra-band contiguous carrier(s) operated in non-SBFD operation. The power spectral density of each interfering signal is set to be the same level.NOTE 5:For FR2-1 WA BS, in-channel adjacent subband blocking requirement assumes 87 dBc coupling loss between interfering BS transmitter and SBFD BS receiver. |  |  |  |  |  |  |


## 12.7Radiated performance requirements for SBFD-capable BS
### 12.7.1General
#### 12.7.1.1Scope and definitions
Radiated performance requirements specify the ability of the SBFD-capable BS type 1-O or BS type 2-O to correctly demodulate radiated signals in various conditions and configurations. Radiated performance requirements are specified at the RIB.
Radiated performance requirements for the BS are specified for the fixed reference channels defined in annex A and the propagation conditions in annex G. The requirements only apply to those FRCs that are supported by the BS.
The radiated performance requirements for SBFD-capable BS type 1-O and for the SBFD-capable BS type 2-O are limited to two OTA demodulation branches as described in clause 12.7.1.2. Conformance requirements can only be tested for 1 or 2 demodulation branches depending on the number of polarizations supported by the BS, with the required SNR applied separately per polarization.
NOTE 1:The BS can support more than 2 demodulation branches, however OTA conformance testing can only be performed for 1 or 2 demodulation branches.
Unless stated otherwise, radiated performance requirements apply for a single carrier only. Radiated performance requirements for a BS supporting CA are defined in terms of single carrier requirements.
In tests performed with signal generators a synchronization signal may be provided from the BS to the signal generator, to enable correct timing of the wanted signal.
Whenever the "RX antennas" term is used for the radiated performance requirements description, it shall refer to the demodulation branches (i.e. not physical antennas of the antenna array).
The SNR used in this clause is specified based on a single carrier and defined as:
SNR = S / N
Where:
Sis the total signal energy in a slot on a RIB.
Nis the noise energy in a bandwidth corresponding to the transmission bandwidth over the duration where signal energy exists on a RIB.
#### 12.7.1.2OTA demodulation branches
Radiated performance requirements are only specified for up to 2 demodulation branches.
If the SBFD-capable BS type 1-O, or the SBFD-capable BS type 2-O uses polarization diversity and has the ability to maintain isolation between the signals for each of the demodulation branches, then radiated performance requirements can be tested for up to two demodulation branches (i.e., 1RX or 2RX test setups). When tested for two demodulation branches, each demodulation branch maps to one polarization.
If the SBFD-capable BS type 1-O, or the SBFD-capable BS type 2-O does not use polarization diversity then radiated performance requirements can only be tested for a single demodulation branch (i.e., 1RX test setup).
#### 12.7.1.3Void
### 12.7.2Performance requirements for PUSCH
#### 12.7.2.1Requirements for BS type 1-O
##### 12.7.2.1.1Requirements for PUSCH with transform precoding disabled
Apply the requirements defined in clause 12.4.2.1 for 2Rx.
##### 12.7.2.1.2Requirements for PUSCH with transform precoding enabled
Apply the requirements defined in clause 12.4.2.2 for 2Rx.
##### 12.7.2.1.3Requirements for UCI multiplexed on PUSCH
Apply the requirements defined in clause 12.4.2.3 for 2Rx.
##### 12.7.2.1.4Enhanced performance requirements for PUSCH with synchronous interference
Apply the requirements defined in clause 12.4.2.4 for 2Rx.
##### 12.7.2.1.5Requirements for PUSCH with UL resource muting
Apply the requirements defined in clause 12.4.2.5 for 2Rx.
#### 12.7.2.2Requirements for BS type 2-O
##### 12.7.2.2.1Requirements for PUSCH with transform precoding disabled
The same general description and requirements as clause 11.2.2.1 is appliable to requirements for PUSCH with transform precoding disabled, where the PUSCH received at at the RIB for SBFD-capable BS type 2-O inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
##### 12.7.2.2.2Requirements for PUSCH with transform precoding enabled
The same general description and requirements as clause 11.2.2.2 is appliable to requirements for PUSCH with transform precoding enabled, where the PUSCH received at at the RIB for SBFD-capable BS type 2-O inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
##### 12.7.2.2.3Requirements for UCI multiplexed on PUSCH
The same general description and requirements as clause 11.2.2.3 is appliable to requirements for UCI multiplexed on PUSCH, where the UCI multiplexed on PUSCH received at at the RIB for SBFD-capable BS type 2-O inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
### 12.7.3Performance requirements for PUCCH
#### 12.7.3.1Requirements for BS type 1-O
##### 12.7.3.1.1DTX to ACK probability
Apply the requirements defined in clause 12.4.3.1
##### 12.7.3.1.2Performance requirements for PUCCH format 0
Apply the requirements defined in clause 12.4.3.2 for 2 Rx.
##### 12.7.3.1.3Performance requirements for PUCCH format 1
Apply the requirements defined in sub-clause 12.4.3.3 for 2Rx.
##### 12.7.3.1.4Performance requirements for PUCCH format 2
Apply the requirements defined in clause 12.4.3.4 for 2Rx.
##### 12.7.3.1.5Performance requirements for PUCCH format 3
Apply the requirements defined in clause 12.4.3.5 for 2Rx.
##### 12.7.3.1.6Performance requirements for PUCCH format 4
Apply the requirements defined in clause 12.4.3.6 for 2Rx.
##### 12.7.3.1.7Performance requirements for multi-slot PUCCH
Apply the requirements defined in clause 12.4.3.7 for 2Rx.
#### 12.7.3.2Requirements for BS type 2-O
##### 12.7.3.2.1DTX to ACK probability
Apply the requirements defined in clause 11.3.2.1.
##### 12.7.3.2.2Performance requirements for PUCCH format 0.
The same general description and requirements as clause 11.3.2.2 is appliable to requirements for PUCCH format 0, where the PUCCH format 0 received at at the RIB for SBFD-capable BS type 2-O inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
##### 12.7.3.2.3Performance requirements for PUCCH format 1.
The same general description and requirements as clause 11.3.2.3 is appliable to requirements for PUCCH format 1, where the PUCCH format 1 received at at the RIB for SBFD-capable BS type 2-O inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
##### 12.7.3.2.4Performance requirements for PUCCH format 2
The same general description and requirements as clause 11.3.2.4 is appliable to requirements for PUCCH format 2, where the PUCCH format 2 received at at the RIB for SBFD-capable BS type 2-O inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
##### 12.7.3.2.5Performance requirements for PUCCH format 3
The same general description and requirements as clause 11.3.2.5 is appliable to requirements for PUCCH format 3, where the PUCCH format 3 received at at the RIB for SBFD-capable BS type 2-O inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
##### 12.7.3.2.6Performance requirements for PUCCH format 4
The same general description and requirements as clause 11.3.2.6 is appliable to requirements for PUCCH format 4, where the PUCCH format 4 received at at the RIB for SBFD-capable BS type 2-O inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
### 12.7.4Performance requirements for PRACH

##### 12.7.4.1.1PRACH False alarm probability
Apply the requirements defined in clause 12.4.4.1 for 2Rx.
##### 12.7.4.1.2PRACH detection requirements
Apply the requirements defined in clause 12.4.4.2 for 2Rx.
#### 12.7.4.2Requirements for BS type 2-O

The same general description and requirements as clause 11.4.2.1 is appliable to requirements for PRACH, where the PRACH received at at the RIB for SBFD-capable BS type 2-O inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.
##### 12.7.4.2.2PRACH detection requirements
The same general description and requirements as clause 11.4.2.2.2 is appliable to requirements for PRACH, where the PRACH received at at the RIB for SBFD-capable BS type 2-O inside the received SBFD UL subband for SBFD operation, and the BS channel bandwidth specified in the tables shall be replaced by SBFD UL subband bandwidth.

The parameters for the reference measurement channels are specified in table A.1-1 for FR1 reference sensitivity level, ACS, in-band blocking, out-of-band blocking, receiver intermodulation, in-channel selectivity, OTA sensitivity, OTA reference sensitivity level, OTA ACS, OTA in-band blocking, OTA out-of-band blocking, OTA receiver intermodulation and OTA in-channel selectivity. The parameters for the band n46, n96 and n102 reference measurement channels are specified in table A.1-1a and A.1-1b for reference sensitivity level, ACS, in-band blocking, out-of-band blocking, receiver intermodulation, in-channel selectivity.
The parameters for the reference measurement channels are specified in table A.1-2 for FR2 OTA reference sensitivity level, OTA ACS, OTA in-band blocking, OTA out-of-band blocking, OTA receiver intermodulation and OTA in-channel selectivity.
Reference measurement channels G-FR2-A1-1 to G-FR2-A1-5 are used for FR2-1 requirements. Reference measurement channels G-FR2-A1-2, G-FR2-A1-3, G-FR2-A1-6 to G-FR2-A1-9 are used for FR2-2 requirements.

# A.3AFixed Reference Channels for performance requirements (QPSK, R=99/1024)
The parameters for the reference measurement channel are specified in table A.3A-1 for FR1 PUSCH performance requirements:
-FRC parameters are specified in table A.3A-1 for FR1 PUSCH with transform precoding disabled, additional DM-RS position = pos1 and 1 transmission layer.
The parameters for the reference measurement channels are specified in table A.3A-2 to A.3A-3 for FR2 PUSCH performance requirements:
-FRC parameters are specified in table A.3A-2 for FR2 PUSCH with transform precoding disabled, additional DM-RS position = pos0 and 1 transmission layer.
-FRC parameters are specified in table A.3A-3 for FR2 PUSCH with transform precoding disabled, additional DM-RS position = pos1 and 1 transmission layer.

# A.5Fixed Reference Channels for performance requirements (64QAM, R=567/1024)
The parameters for the reference measurement channels are specified in table A.5-2, table A.5-5, table A.5-6 , and table A.5-7 for FR1 PUSCH performance requirements:
-FRC parameters are specified in table A.5-2 for FR1 PUSCH with transform precoding disabled, Additional DM-RS position = pos1 and 1 transmission layer.
-FRC parameters are specified in table A.5-5 for FR1 interlaced PUSCH with transform precoding disabled, Additional DM-RS position = pos1 and 1 transmission layer.
-FRC parameters are specified in table A.5-6 for FR1 PUSCH with transform precoding disabled for ATG scenario, Additional DM-RS position = pos1 and 2 transmission layers.
-FRC parameters are specified in table A.5-7 for FR1 PUSCH with transform precoding disabled, Additional DM-RS position = pos1 and 3 transmission layers.
The parameters for the reference measurement channels are specified in table A.5-3 to table A.5-4 for FR2-1 PUSCH performance requirements:
-FRC parameters are specified in table A.5-3 for FR2-1 PUSCH with transform precoding disabled, Additional DM-RS position = pos0 and 1 transmission layer.
-FRC parameters are specified in table A.5-4 for FR2-1 PUSCH with transform precoding disabled, Additional DM-RS position = pos1 and 1 transmission layer.
The parameters for the reference measurement channels are specified in table A.5-4A for FR2-2 PUSCH performance requirements:
-FRC parameters are specified in table A.5-4A for FR2-2 PUSCH with transform precoding disabled, Additional DM-RS position = pos1 and 1 transmission layer.

###### Annex B (normative):Error Vector Magnitude (FR1)
# B.1Reference point for measurement
The EVM shall be measured at the point after the FFT and a zero-forcing (ZF) equalizer in the receiver, as depicted in figure B.1-1 below.

Figure B.1-1: Reference point for EVM measurement
# B.2Basic unit of measurement
The basic unit of EVM measurement is defined over one slot in the time domain and  subcarriers in the frequency domain:

where
T is the set of symbols with the considered modulation scheme being active within the slot,
is the set of subcarriers within the  subcarriers with the considered modulation scheme being active in symbol t,
is the ideal signal reconstructed by the measurement equipment in accordance with relevant Tx models,
is the modified signal under test defined in annex B.3.
NOTE:Although the basic unit of measurement is one slot, the equalizer is calculated over 10 ms measurement interval to reduce the impact of noise in the reference signals. The boundaries of the 10 ms measurement intervals need not be aligned with radio frame boundaries.
# B.3Modified signal under test
Implicit in the definition of EVM is an assumption that the receiver is able to compensate a number of transmitter impairments. The signal under test is equalized and decoded according to:

where
is the time domain samples of the signal under test.
is the sample timing difference between the FFT processing window in relation to nominal timing of the ideal signal. Note that two timing offsets are determined, the corresponding EVM is measured and the maximum used as described in annex B.7.
is the RF frequency offset.
is the phase response of the TX chain.
is the amplitude response of the TX chain.

# B.4Estimation of frequency offset
The observation period for determining the frequency offset  shall be 1 slot.
# B.5Estimation of time offset
## B.5.1General
The observation period for determining the sample timing difference shall be 1 slot.
In the following  represents the middle sample of the EVM window of length W (defined in annex B.5.2) or the last sample of the first window half if W is even.
is estimated so that the EVM window of length W is centred on the measured cyclic prefix of the considered OFDM symbol. To minimize the estimation error the timing shall be based on demodulation reference signals. To limit time distortion of any transmit filter the reference signals in the 1 outer RBs are not taken into account in the timing estimation
Two values for  are determined:
and
where  if W is odd and  if W is even.
When the cyclic prefix length varies from symbol to symbol then T shall be further restricted to the subset of symbols with the considered modulation scheme being active and with the considered cyclic prefix length type.

# B.6Estimation of TX chain amplitude and frequency response parameters
The equalizer coefficients and  are determined as follows:
1.Calculate the complex ratios (amplitude and phase) of the post-FFT acquired signal  and the post-FFT ideal signal , for each reference signal, over 10ms measurement interval. This process creates a set of complex ratios:

Where the post-FFT ideal signal  is constructed by the measuring equipment according to the relevant TX specifications, using the following parameters: i.e. nominal demodulation reference signals, (all other modulation symbols are set to 0 V), nominal carrier frequency,  nominal amplitude and phase for each applicable subcarrier, nominal timing.
2.Perform time averaging at each reference signal subcarrier of the complex ratios, the time-averaging length is 10ms measurement interval. Prior to the averaging of the phases  an unwrap operation must be performed according to the following definition: The unwrap operation corrects the radian phase angles of  by adding multiples of 2*PI when absolute phase jumps between consecutive time instances ti are greater than or equal to the jump tolerance of PI radians. This process creates an average amplitude and phase for each reference signal subcarrier (i.e. every second subcarrier).

Where N is the number of reference signal; time-domain locations ti from  for each reference signal subcarrier .
3.The equalizer coefficients for amplitude and phase  and  at the reference signal subcarriers are obtained by computing the moving average in the frequency domain of the time-averaged reference signal subcarriers, i.e. every second subcarrier. The moving average window size is 19 and averaging is over the DM-RS subcarriers in the allocated RBs. For DM-RS subcarriers at or near the edge of the channel , or when the number of available DM-RS subcarriers within a set of contiguously allocated RBs is smaller than the moving average window size, the window size is reduced accordingly as per figure B.6-1.
4.Perform linear interpolation from the equalizer coefficients  and  to compute coefficients ,  for each subcarrier.

Figure B.6-1: Reference subcarrier smoothing in the frequency domain

For FDD the averaging in the time domain equals the  slot duration of the 10 ms measurement interval from the equalizer estimation step.
